question: Describe the structure of the helical tube bundle in the steam generator.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility. It references relevant sections of the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and lists the specific alloys and material specifications for the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, bolting, and heater sheaths.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, detailing the integration of the reactor, steam generators, pressurizer, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV). It outlines the configuration and boundaries of the steam generator system, emphasizing that the steam generators are integral to the RPV and form part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and share the vessel’s pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the reactor pressure vessel itself forming the shell and pressure boundary for the steam generators. The design ensures separation between the primary and secondary sides, with specific components forming the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

edges.description: Section A provides detailed descriptions of specific pressurizer components and their materials, which are part of the broader system components listed in Section B's general overview of the reactor coolant system design.

Section A describes the pressurizer components and materials within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B provides a general overview of all major components inside the reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators, thus structurally situating the pressurizer (from A) and the steam generators (from B) as co-located subsystems within the same vessel.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, while Section B focuses specifically on the steam generators, which are one of the components listed in Section A.
